message:
feat(runtime): Add region heap allocator and RRef type for Phase 1 parallel tasks

Implements the core memory infrastructure for Phase 1 multi-threaded execution:

## Region Heap (`src/runtime/region_heap.rs`)

A per-region heap allocator that enables safe parallel task execution by
ensuring allocated data outlives all tasks in the region:

- Type-erased storage with generation-based indices (HeapIndex)
- No unsafe code - safety through structured concurrency guarantees
- Automatic reclamation when region closes to quiescence
- Global allocation counter for testing memory reclamation
- HeapRef<T> typed wrapper for ergonomic access

Key design decisions:
- Indices don't expose memory addresses (preserves lab determinism)
- Generation counters prevent ABA problems on slot reuse
- Bulk reclamation on Drop (no per-allocation overhead)

## RRef Type (`src/types/rref.rs`)

Region-owned reference type for Send tasks to safely reference region-heap data:

- RRef<T> stores RegionId + HeapIndex, is Copy + Send when T: Send
- Access requires passing the RegionRecord (runtime lookup, validation)
- RRefAccess trait implemented on RegionRecord for ergonomic access
- Full test coverage including Send/Sync compile-time checks

## Integration

- RegionRecord gains heap_alloc(), heap_get(), heap_with(), heap_len() methods
- RRefAccess trait provides rref_get() and rref_with() for typed access
- Module exports added to src/runtime/mod.rs and src/types/mod.rs

This enables the following Phase 1 pattern:
```rust
let index = region.heap_alloc(expensive_data);
let rref = RRef::new(region_id, index);
scope.spawn(move |cx| async move {
    let data = rref.get_via_region(&region_record)?;
    process(data).await
});
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
